target: Overview falsifiability module fixes: stale Chapman range, denominator reconciliation note, condition-1 framing
tldr_evidence: ISS-1655 (factual error): eg-falsifiability Eclipse pre-commitment paragraph at line 675 uses stale numbers: "5-20 nT Chapman range" (should be 5-25 nT per §4.2.3 and §4.2.6), "dome's -17 to -21 nT range" (actually W004 reaches -26.2 nT at Hartland), and "only values above 20 nT would favor the dome" (§4.2.6 says discriminating window is 25-26.2 nT). Fix: update these three figures to match §4.2.6. | ISS-1653 (denominator mismatch): falsifiability module caption says "69 claimed wins" while verdict bar shows 71 total WINs (2 are non-dome entries excluded from the 69 count). No reconciliation note. Fix: add a parenthetical clarifying that "69" excludes 2 non-dome WINs. | ISS-1656 (framing): "0 / 69 met" for condition 1 implies all 69 were attempted and failed; only 2 attempted. Sub-text "2 attempted · 0 succeed" helps but headline misleads. Fix: change aria-label to be precise about "0 of 2 attempted succeed" framing, keeping visual "0 / 69 met" if decider prefers not to change visible text.

patches.description: Fix stale Chapman/dome numbers in eg-falsifiability eclipse pre-commitment (ISS-1655)
patches.find_pattern: Based on the Chapman mechanism (the standard physics explanation for eclipse-induced magnetic effects, documented since 1933), we expect anomalies of 5–20 nT in magnitude. If the dome's predictions are confirmed and fall outside the Chapman-mechanism range — that is, if the measured values match the dome’s −17 to −21 nT range but not the 5–20 nT Chapman range — we will upgrade the relevant WIN. If they fall within both ranges, the result supports standard physics, not dome geometry, because the dome's prediction values were derived by scaling Chapman-mechanism data. We note that the ranges overlap substantially: any measurement between 17 and 20 nT in magnitude falls within both the dome's range and the Chapman range, and only values above 20 nT would favor the dome. The discriminating window is approximately 1 nT
patches.replace_pattern: Based on the Chapman mechanism (the standard physics explanation for eclipse-induced magnetic effects, documented since 1933), we expect anomalies of 5–25 nT in magnitude. If the dome's predictions are confirmed and fall outside the Chapman-mechanism range — that is, if measured values exceed 25 nT but remain within the dome's W004 upper bound (~26.2 nT at Hartland) — we will upgrade the relevant WIN. If they fall within both ranges (i.e., at or below 25 nT), the result supports standard physics, not dome geometry, because the dome's prediction values were derived by scaling Chapman-mechanism data. The dome's W004 baseline extends from −10 to −26.2 nT across stations; the BOU baseline cluster (−5 to −13 nT) falls entirely inside the Chapman range. The discriminating window is approximately 1 nT
patches.note: This updates the eg-falsifiability eclipse pre-commitment to match the formal commitment numbers in §4.2.6. The old numbers (5-20 nT Chapman, -17 to -21 nT dome range) predated the dual-baseline analysis in §4.2.3.

patches.description: Add reconciliation note to falsifiability module caption clarifying 69 vs 71 WIN count (ISS-1653)
patches.find_pattern: Four conditions assessed against the dome's 69 claimed wins — any one is sufficient to upgrade a verdict.
patches.replace_pattern: Four conditions assessed against the dome's 69 claimed wins (excludes 2 non-dome structural comparators; see verdict summary) — any one is sufficient to upgrade a verdict.

patches.description: Update aria-label for condition 1 status to clarify attempt vs failure (ISS-1656)
patches.find_pattern: aria-label="Status: 0 of 69 claims met; 2 attempted (WIN-044, WIN-057), 0 succeed"
patches.replace_pattern: aria-label="Status: 0 of 69 claims produce a dome-only result; 2 attempted (WIN-044, WIN-057), neither yields a discriminating dome-specific value"

tldr_verdict: Three fixes to Overview falsifiability module: stale Chapman range corrected to 5-25 nT (matching §4.2.6); denominator note added (69 excludes 2 non-dome WINs); condition-1 aria-label reworded to avoid conflating "did not attempt" with "attempted and failed".
